faqs.a: Bulimia nervosa is a serious eating disorder built around a cycle. A person eats in a way that feels out of control, then tries to undo it or compensate, often driven by intense fear about weight and body shape. The cycle tends to repeat and can become deeply entrenched.

faqs.q: What is the difference between bulimia and anorexia?
faqs.a: Both are serious eating disorders involving fear about weight and body shape. Bulimia centers on a recurring cycle of eating that feels out of control followed by attempts to compensate, while anorexia centers on food restriction driven by an intense fear of weight gain.

faqs.q: Can you have bulimia without being underweight?
faqs.a: Yes. Bulimia isn't defined by body size. People across a wide range of body types can have it, and many don't look unwell from the outside. It's treatable, and recovery is very possible.
